data.title: Nokia in charts: Q4 sales rise across all segments and regions Q/Q except Nokia Technologies
Galan Lithium secures A$40M to support project expansion
data.description:
• Galan Lithium (GLNLF) secured A$40 million in commitments from institutional and sophisticated investors, including major shareholder Clean Elements Fund, via a placement at A$0.41 per share.
  • The directors have separately committed to subscribe for an additional A$1M subject to shareholder approval.
  • The institutional placement involves two tranches: Tranche 1 (~86.6M shares) settles in early February 2026, while Tranche 2 (13.4M shares, including director participation)
  • The proceeds will be used to expand production at HMW from the currently planned 4 ktpa LCE to 5.2 ktpa LCE, fund exploration activities at Greenbushes South, and provide working capital.

data.title: 37 Capital announces private placement
data.description:
• 37 Capital (HHHEF) proposed a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $250,000 by the issuance of up to 2.5M units of the company at a price of $0.10 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one share purchase warrant to acquire one common share of the company at a price of $0.15 per share for a period of 3 years.
  • The funds raised from the financing will be used towards general working capital.

data.title: Canon GAAP EPS of ¥367.25, revenue of ¥4624.73B; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Canon press release (CAJPY): FY GAAP EPS of ¥367.25.
  • Revenue of ¥4624.73B (+2.5% Y/Y).

data.title: Russia's Lukoil to sell international subsidiary to Carlyle
data.description:
• Russia’s Lukoil PJSC (LUKOY) on Thursday said that it agreed to sell most of its international assets to U.S. investment firm Carlyle Group (CG).
  • The deal doesn’t include assets in Kazakhstan, the energy producer said Thursday in a statement.
  • Lukoil said the transaction is subject to regulatory approvals, and it’s continuing talks with other potential buyers. Financial terms weren’t disclosed.

data.title: Takeda Pharmaceutical Non-GAAP EPS of ¥428.00, revenue of ¥3411.2B; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Takeda Pharmaceutical press release (TAK): for the nine-months ended it reported Non-GAAP EPS of ¥428.00.
  • Revenue of ¥3411.2B (-3.3% Y/Y).

  • FY2025 Outlook

    Updating Full Year Management Guidance for Revenue and Forecasts

data.title: Nokia Oyj declares EUR 0.03 dividend
data.description:
• Nokia Oyj (NOK) declares EUR 0.03/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 2.1%
  • Payable Feb. 12; for shareholders of record Feb. 3; ex-div Feb. 3.
  • See NOK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: United Acquisition prices $100M IPO at $10 per share
Roche Holding Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 19.46, revenue of CHF 61.52B; issues FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Roche Holding press release (RHHBY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 19.46.
  • Revenue of CHF 61.52B (+1.7% Y/Y).
  • Sales in the fourth quarter increased by 8%, reflecting the positive momentum.

  • Outlook for 2026

    Roche expects an increase in Group sales in the mid single digit range (CER) for 2026. Core earnings per share are targeted to develop in the high single digit range (CER). Roche expects to further increase its dividend in Swiss francs.

data.title: ING Group GAAP EPS of €0.48, total income of €5.8B; gives strong outlook for 2026 and upgrades outlook for 2027
data.description:
• ING Group press release (ING): Q4 GAAP EPS of €0.48.
  • Total Income of €5.8B (+7.2% Y/Y).
  • Increase of 352,000 mobile primary customers in the fourth quarter, with growth in all markets.
  • Net core deposits grew by €9.5 billion in 4Q2025.
  • Strong outlook for 2026 and upgraded outlook for 2027 • Our 2025 performance lays the base for a strong outlook for 2026 and upgraded financial outlook for 2027 • Return on tangible equity (ROTE) outlook of >14% for 2026 and an outlook of >15% for 2027.
  • Outlook for 2026 1) "Based on current assumptions and scenarios, we expect a total income of around €24 billion in 2026. This outlook is supported by volume growth and an anticipated 5-10% increase in fee income. Total operating expenses (excluding incidental cost items) are projected to be in the range of €12.6-€12.8 billion in 2026. For the full year 2026, we expect an effective tax rate of 29-31%. We maintain our CET1 capital ratio guidance at ~13%. In addition, we will transition from a return on equity (ROE) metric to return on tangible equity (ROTE, further explained on page 15). The definitional change results in an uplift of approximately 40 basis points, and ROTE is expected to exceed 14% for full-year 2026."
  • Outlook for 2027 1) "Based on current assumptions and scenarios, we are announcing an upgraded outlook for 2027. We expect total income to exceed €25 billion, including more than €5 billion in fee income. And we anticipate operating expenses (excluding incidental cost items) of around €13 billion, an effective tax rate of 29-31% and an ROTE above 15%. We maintain our CET1 capital ratio guidance at ~13%."
